summary: Verified (1) importer _relativize_assets fix: 'ivorydigital' site now renders FULLY STYLED in the editor canvas — serif Cormorant Garamond H1 (62.4px), Jost body on ivory bg #f8f7f1, small Ivory Digital script logo, gold accent on 'START YOUR FREE 7-DAY TRIAL' button + 'BESPOKE BOOKING SYSTEMS' eyebrow; stylesheet /api/asset/ivorydigital/assets/css/style.v3.css returns 200 text/css; base=/api/asset/ivorydigital/. NO massive/unstyled icons. (2) Templates tab is present and functional — Admin settings > tab-templates lists 'Used Cars / Stock page' with BUILT-IN badge and description; superadmin sees '+ Add a template' (add-template-btn). (3) AddPageModal blank/template toggle works on car-demo: mode-blank + mode-template both present; template mode reveals addpage-template dropdown and addpage-enquiry-email input. (4) car-demo regression: still renders styled (Sora heading, Manrope body, Apex Motors header, 0 root-absolute asset refs, base=/api/asset/car-demo/, style.css loaded). All 5 review items PASS.

test_results.ivorydigital_styled_editor_canvas: PASS — h1='Beautiful booking software, built just for your bridal boutique.' rendered in Cormorant Garamond 62.4px; body font=Jost on rgb(248,247,241) ivory bg; small logo.png rendered at 560x184 natural; base=https://.../api/asset/ivorydigital/; 2 stylesheets loaded (Google Fonts + style.v3.css both 200 text/css). No oversized icons — screenshot iv_editor.png shows fully styled hero + product cards.
test_results.no_broken_root_absolute_asset_refs: PASS — style.v3.css fetched 200 text/css from iframe context. The 23 remaining root-absolute refs are nav links (href='/about/', '/pricing/', '/faq/' etc) — correctly LEFT UNTOUCHED per _relativize_assets design (only asset file extensions rewrite). 0 root-absolute *asset* (css/js/img) refs.
test_results.templates_tab_present_and_lists_builtin: PASS — tab-templates count=1; clicking opens 'Used Cars / Stock page' with 'BUILT-IN' badge and full description; add-template-btn visible (superadmin).
test_results.addpage_modal_template_toggle_regression: PASS — mode-blank + mode-template segmented toggle present on car-demo AddPageModal; selecting template mode reveals addpage-template dropdown and addpage-enquiry-email input.
test_results.car_demo_still_renders_styled: PASS — base=/api/asset/car-demo/; Apex Motors header (Our stock/Why us/Finance/Contact/01234 567 890) + hero 'Hand-picked used cars, honestly priced.' in Sora; Manrope body on dark #0c0e12 bg; amber 'View our stock' button; style.css?v=20260719 loaded; 0 root-absolute asset refs.

evidence_screenshots: /app/.screenshots/iv_editor.png (ivorydigital home in editor — styled: serif Cormorant Garamond, ivory bg, gold accents, small logo, dashboard screenshot)
/app/.screenshots/templates_tab.png (Templates tab with Used Cars/Stock page BUILT-IN + Add a template)
/app/.screenshots/cardemo.png (car-demo regression — Apex Motors header, Sora heading, amber accents)
critical_code_review_comments: _relativize_assets is correctly scoped: only src=, srcset=, url(), and hrefs ending in asset extensions get relativized — nav <a href='/about/'> links are preserved so in-editor navigation is unaffected. Good.

Since a self-hosted user reported they can't see Templates and still see unstyled ivorydigital, the code in this preview is correct — the issue on their side is a stale Docker frontend build. Recommend main agent instruct the user to rebuild frontend (yarn build) and re-run their importer to re-ingest with _relativize_assets applied to existing pages.

success_rate.backend: not directly tested (auth-gated; verified via UI + iframe fetches)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The Admin settings modal does NOT close via Escape key — use the × close button [inside .modal-overlay button] or click outside. tab-templates already verified in iteration_13 & 14. To re-ingest ivorydigital after importer change, main agent uses backend re-ingest endpoint (already done). The 23 root-absolute nav-link refs remaining in the ivorydigital iframe HTML are intentional — those are page links (/about/, /pricing/), not assets.
rca of the issue: N/A — the code fix is verified working in preview. User's real-world complaint (massive icons, no Templates) is a deployment-side stale build issue on their self-hosted Docker instance, NOT a code bug.
